---
title: "GTPCNZ"
subtitle: "Primary care funding architecture model and dashboard"
---

GTPCNZ is a public-facing reporting layer for a source-informed model scaffold about primary care funding architecture in Aotearoa New Zealand.

## Start here

1. [Read the public report](reports/primary_care_architecture.qmd)
2. [Open the dashboard guide](docs/REPORTS-AND-DASHBOARD.md)
3. [Read the model card](docs/calibration/model-card-v1.7.2.md)
4. [Read the claim boundaries](docs/launch/claim-boundaries-v1.7.2.md)
5. [Review the evidence tracker](docs/public-site/evidence-tracker-public-v1.8.1.md)
6. [Review calibration readiness](docs/public-site/calibration-readiness-page-v1.8.1.md)

## Current status

This is a **source-informed, parameterised policy model scaffold**.

> This is a source-informed parameterised scaffold and educational explainer. It is not a real-data calibrated forecast and should not be used to claim precise fiscal savings, hospital-demand reductions, workforce effects, or implementation impacts.

## Current thesis

Capitation should be retained for continuity, enrolment, baseline viability and population responsibility. Eligible primary medical activity should also have an uncapped, scheduled, rules-based fee-for-service stream, controlled through item rules, provider scope, clinical governance, documentation, audit, co-payment protections and place-based accountability.

Short version:

> Uncapped does not mean uncontrolled.

## Public surfaces

- GitHub Pages: this site.
- Streamlit: interactive explainer and scenario dashboard.
- Repository: source code, outputs, model card, claim boundaries and tests.

## Local commands

Render the website:

```bash
quarto render
```

Run the dashboard:

```bash
streamlit run streamlit_app.py
```
